meta.idea_engine_pin: 4d1b86b (proposal landed via idea-engine PR #436, merged 2026-07-15T09:19:39Z; grounding pin e17ebe7)
meta.grounding_pin: idea-engine e17ebe7d733ecc431556b0e8e3d6788b27783b34 (the dedup-sweep HEAD; the only read the head takes)
meta.hermetic: the runner reads ONLY this fixtures.json; zero repo/network reads at verdict time. Every model constant is invented-but-pinned in the PROPOSAL 072 block / idea file and copied verbatim below; the DECISION arms are seedless exact fractions.Fraction with ZERO repo/network reads (the P017-P071 hermetic precedent). There is no external grounding pin to re-verify: the whole world is the pinned cost law T(n,p) = 1/n + 1 - (1-p)^n and its committed grids. Values disclosed under anchors.disclosed_* are the drafter's expected landing, re-derived from scratch and NOT trusted (the P048-P071 exact-arm norm).

model.cost_law: Dorfman two-stage screening, pool size n: one test per disjoint pool of size n; a negative pool clears all n items, a positive pool retests all n members individually (n more tests). Items defective i.i.d. Bernoulli(p), q = 1 - p. Cost metric T(n, p) = expected TESTS PER ITEM = 1/n + 1 - q^n for n >= 2; T(1) = 1 exactly (individual baseline). Tests are PERFECT (no false neg/pos, no dilution). Test count is the ONLY cost.
model.outcome_tree: T(n, p) = (1/n) * sum_{k=0..n} C(n,k) p^k q^(n-k) * cost(k), cost(0) = 1 (negative pool, one test), cost(k>=1) = 1 + n (positive pool: pool test + n retests). Arm B recomputes T from this tree independently and must equal Arm A exactly.
model.falsifiability_world: delete the pool test's own 1/n cost (the accounting slip 'the pool test is basically free'): T~(n, p) = 1 - q^n < 1 for every n >= 2 and every p < 1, so the wall VANISHES and the verdict flips to APPROVE outright. The entire mechanism lives in the 1/n term. This is a NAMED reporting world, never a decision code path (F1: the 1/n term is present in every decision T).

theorems.T1_wall: pooling at size n beats individual testing iff T(n,q) < 1 iff q^n > 1/n iff q > n^(-1/n). The most forgiving pool size minimizes n^(-1/n); on integers that minimum is n = 3, certified by INTEGER-POWER comparisons (n^(-1/n) < m^(-1/m) iff n^m > m^n): 3^2=9>8=2^3, 3^4=81>64=4^3, 3^5=243>125=5^3, and (n+1)^n < n^(n+1) for every n>=3 (n^(-1/n) strictly increasing from n=3 on). Hence pooling at ANY size helps iff q > 3^(-1/3), i.e. iff p < p* = 1 - 3^(-1/3) ~ 0.306566. At a rational p the certificate is one multiplication: is 3q^3 > 1? Boundary law T(3,q) = 1 iff 3q^3 = 1 (symbolic).
theorems.T2_pair_dominance: the exact polynomial identity T(2,q) - T(3,q) = (q - 2/3)^2 (q + 1/3) + 1/54: the pair-pool is strictly dominated by the triple at EVERY prevalence, minimum gap exactly 1/54 attained ONLY at q = 2/3 (p = 1/3) - a named knife-edge equality cell. Either batch >= 3 or don't batch at all.
theorems.T3_degeneracy: the companion identity T(2,q) - T(4,q) = (q^2 - 1/2)^2: pool 4 weakly dominates pool 2 everywhere with a genuine MARGIN-0 knife-edge at q^2 = 1/2, where both pools simultaneously break even against individual testing (q2* = q4* = 2^(-1/2), because 2^4 = 4^2 - the unique integer coincidence in the n^(1/n) family). q^2 = 1/2 is irrational, so no rational grid point lands on it (the family's one true zero, certified symbolically).

committed_constants.cost_law_note: T(n, p) = 1/n + 1 - (1-p)^n for n >= 2; T(1) = 1. All p exact rationals.
committed_constants.cost_metric: expected tests per item; test count is the only cost

pool_grid.note: n in {1..64}; T(1) = 1 is the individual baseline

practical_subgrid.note: the R1 damage clause quantifies min over n in {2..8}

grids.identity_grid_note: q in {k/100 : k = 0..100} union {2/3}; the appended point 2/3 is the T2 equality cell

decision.reading: evaluation order REJECT FIRST; both independently-written decision evaluators (Arm A numbers, Arm B numbers) must agree on the ruling token; a gate (F1-F6) failure is reported first-class beside the ruling but, with REJECT already fired on exact arithmetic, the INVALID stage is never reached (the P059-P071 REJECT-first precedent)

reject.R1: at p = 2/5: T(n, 2/5) > 1 for EVERY n in {2..64} (the strict wall clause) AND min over the practical grid n in {2..8} of T(n, 2/5) >= 21/20 (every practical pool wastes >= 5%)
reject.R2: T(2, q) - T(3, q) >= 1/54 at every q on the identity grid, with the identity T(2,q) - T(3,q) = (q - 2/3)^2 (q + 1/3) + 1/54 verified by exact coefficient match (equality attained ONLY at q = 2/3)
reject.R3: at p = 1/100: T*(1/100) <= 1/4 (pooling saves >= 75% where the folk belief lives)

decision.invalid: any F1-F6 gate failure (controls misbehaving - reported, no ruling on its own; REJECT-first means a fired REJECT is unaffected)
decision.approve: min over n in {2..64} of T(n, 2/5) <= 1 AND T(2, q) <= T(3, q) at some identity-grid q (mutually exclusive with REJECT by arithmetic)
decision.null_axes: wall-straddle (R1 holds but R3 fails, or the >= 21/20 clause fails while the strict clause holds); identity failure without gate failure (a T2/T3 coefficient mismatch - the corrected law is the finding); margin-erosion (min over {2..8} of T(n,2/5) in (1, 21/20)); twin-arm disagreement surviving the INVALID diagnosis

gates.F1: model identities: T(1) = 1 exactly; probability normalization sum_k C(n,k) p^k q^(n-k) = 1 at every grid cell; the stage-1 test ALWAYS counted (the 1/n term present in every decision T); the rearrangement T(n,p) = 1 + 1/n - q^n
gates.F2: the three structure theorems exact at every cell: (a) WALL integer-power certificate chain (3^2>2^3, 3^4>4^3, 3^5>5^3, 2^4=4^2, (n+1)^n < n^(n+1) for n in {3..64}) establishing argmin over the grid of n^(-1/n) at n = 3; the wall statement at every rational grid p: pooling helps for some n in {2..64} iff 3q^3 > 1; the boundary law T(3,q) = 1 iff 3q^3 = 1; (b) PAIR-DOMINANCE identity by exact coefficient match AND on the identity grid, equality exactly and only at q = 2/3; (c) 2-4 DEGENERACY identity by exact coefficient match AND on the identity grid; break-even q2* = q4* = 2^(-1/2) certified via 2^4 = 4^2; the margin-0 knife-edge named symbolically
gates.F3: census anchors (exact reference values): the helping cells n*(p) and T*(p) below the wall; the above-wall summaries. See anchors.census_helping (RAN, gated exact) and anchors.disclosed_above_wall (the drafter's expected-landing summaries, re-derived and compared as reporting per C3 - see the disclosed-anomaly convention)
gates.F4: the hand world: T(2, 1/2) = 5/4 exactly (both clean prob 1/4 -> 1 test; else -> 3 tests; E = (1/4)*1 + (3/4)*3 = 5/2 per pool = 5/4 per item)
gates.F5: degeneracy controls: p = 0 -> T(n) = 1/n exactly; p = 1 -> T(n) = 1 + 1/n exactly; T(n, p) strictly increasing in p at fixed n on the identity grid
gates.F6: battery: Arm B reproduces every Arm-A number exactly (outcome tree vs closed form, independent n* search, independent wall check q^n vs 1/n); twin decision evaluators agree on the ruling token; Arm-R draw-count sentinels (n Bernoulli draws per pool) counted and asserted; presentation seed 20261642 read by presentation legs only; aux seed 20261643 never read; stdout + results.json byte-identical across two process runs

disclosed_above_wall.note: the drafter's expected-landing SUMMARIES for the two above-wall decision/census cells. RE-DERIVED from scratch and compared as reporting (C3): a mismatch is a first-class ANOMALY, never a silent gate. Both summaries name n = 3 as the argmin of T over a POOL grid at an above-wall prevalence.

p_third_grid_min.claim: min over {2..64} of T(n,1/3) = 28/27 at n = 3

best_batch_waste_pct.claim: the BEST batch above the wall (p=2/5) wastes 11.7%

arm_r.note: seeded, REPORTING-ONLY - NO statistical gate rides Arm R; its only gates are the draw-count sentinel (n Bernoulli draws per pool) and byte-identical reproducibility
arm_r.seed_note: the drafter's registered set 20261640-643 IS the session seed set; NO new allocation. 20261634-639 is the disclosed in-flight gap, unused. High-water after this slice: 20261643 (reserved; highest READ 20261642).

conventions.C1: half-open pool accounting: cost(0) = 1 test (negative pool), cost(k>=1) = 1 + n tests (positive pool retests all n); metric is tests PER ITEM (divide pool cost by n)
conventions.C2: Arm B is INDEPENDENTLY WRITTEN: it recomputes T from the binomial outcome tree, runs its own n* search, and does its own wall check (q^n vs 1/n directly), never reusing Arm A's closed form or certificate; the second decision evaluator recomputes every decision number from Arm B alone
conventions.C3: disclosed-anchor comparison: values under anchors.disclosed_* are the drafter's expected landing, re-derived from scratch and compared as REPORTING; a mismatch raises a first-class ANOMALY (not a gate check failure), the corrected value pinned - the P048-P071 exact-arm 'do not trust the disclosed landing' norm, and the V084-A2 disclosed()-anomaly mechanism
conventions.C4: the falsifiability world (delete 1/n) is computed as a NAMED reporting demonstration that APPROVE is reachable; it is never a decision code path (F1 keeps the 1/n term in every decision T)
conventions.C5: the margin ledger (V083 practice): a grid-wide margin-0 census over the decision geometry, with a gate that NO decision clause rests on a margin-0 cell; the R2 q=2/3 equality and the T3 q^2=1/2 zero are registered knife-edges, disclosed not discovered
conventions.C6: Arm-R estimand pinned: mean tests-per-item over N items simulated under the exact two-stage protocol; draw discipline = n Bernoulli(p) draws per pool, counted and asserted; no statistical gate
conventions.C7: seed constructor registry (C8-of-V084 kin): the ONLY RNGs constructed are seed_main, seed_stability, seed_presentation in pinned order; aux seed asserted never read
conventions.C8: print discipline (the P071 hazard, inherited): decision numbers gated by in-memory Fraction equality FIRST, printed second; the p=1/100 cell numerators are ~23 digits, well under CPython's 4300-digit int-print default - no large-rank hazard, but the fixture still gates before printing
conventions.C9: canonical serialization: results.json written with sort_keys, ensure_ascii, indent=2, trailing newline, newline='\n' - byte-identical across process runs
